name: Enlarge/Reduce
description: For the duration, the spell enlarges or reduces a creature you can see within range. The mode is driven by the target: cast on yourself (or an ally) to Enlarge; cast on an enemy to Reduce (unwilling, so a Constitution save negates). Enlarge: the target grows one size category to Large, gains Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and its weapon hits deal an extra 1d4 damage. Reduce: the target shrinks one category, has Disadvantage on Strength checks/saves, and its weapon hits deal 1d4 less (minimum 1). Engine: Enlarge is wired as a caster self-buff (size, STR-check/save Advantage via Enhance-Ability + save-advantage, +1d4 weapon damage); Reduce applies the `reduced` condition (−1d4 to the creature's weapon hits) on a failed CON save. STR-save Disadvantage on a reduced enemy and the ally-Enlarge damage rider are descriptive. The `effect.onFail` list is the Concentration-end cleanup, not a generic save application (this spell is resolved by its own handler).
